beats.text: Somewhere beneath this {setting_noun}, a {container_noun} was sealed against those who would use its {knowledge_plural} irresponsibly. That judgment was made by someone who no longer breathes. Descend.
The guardian was the first lock. You did not pick it — you broke it. The {knowledge_plural} {discovery_verb} beyond do not know yet that their keeper has fallen.

The deeper archive. {knowledge_plural|capitalize} the surface world never found, never missed. The {atmosphere} down here is different — older. Something has been reading these {knowledge_plural} for centuries.
You have recovered enough to be dangerous. The {container_noun} did not hold every {knowledge_noun} — it held the ones that mattered. You are close to those now.

The last chamber. What was buried here was buried with intention. The {guardian_noun} that remains was not left by accident. It was left because whoever sealed this place knew someone like you would come.

The archive is open. The {knowledge_plural} you carried out — {discovery_verb} from {material} — will change what the surface world believes it knows. Whether that is a gift or a catastrophe depends on what you do next.

The archive closes over you. Another reader, lost in the stacks. The {knowledge_plural} you reached are already forgetting you were here — sealed back into {material} and {atmosphere}, as if you never came.

branches.text: The {danger_noun} is working. Even the archive tries to protect itself.
The {knowledge_plural} respond to mastery. The {atmosphere} brightens fractionally. Something in the archive approves.
The second level of any archive contains what was deemed too dangerous for the first. The {guardian_noun} here were chosen accordingly.
